types.name: FusionRunnerVisibilityControlsWindow
types.xmlDoc: This window contains controls for each active NetworkRunner (see Multi-Peer) including UI toggles for runner SetVisible() and ProvideInput members. NetworkRunner and Player Objects can be pinged in the hierarchy. FusionStats creation shortcuts are provided for convenience as well.

properties.name: Instance
properties.xmlDoc: Window instance.

methods.name: ShowWindow
methods.xmlDoc: Create window instance.

methods.name: ShowButton
methods.xmlDoc: Draw buttons on toolbar. Automatically called by unity.

types.name: FusionEditorSkin
types.xmlDoc: An editor scriptable object that stores UI skins and different Unity inspectors and custom windows.

events.name: OnProjectileCreated
events.type: Action<int, int, string, float, float, float>

methods.name: TriggerHelpRequested
methods.xmlDoc: Emite un pedido de ayuda al escuadron. priority 1 = lider atacado (urgente), priority 2 = aliado atacado.

methods.name: TriggerUnitDowned
methods.xmlDoc: Notifica al escuadron que un soldado ha caido.

methods.name: TriggerUnitRevived
methods.xmlDoc: Notifica al escuadron que un soldado fue revivido.

types.name: GestorTexto
types.xmlDoc: Este script gestiona la visualizaci�n del texto en la UI.

methods.name: BuscarTextoRecogido
methods.xmlDoc: Busca el objeto de texto en la escena por su nombre.

methods.name: MostrarTexto
methods.xmlDoc: Muestra el texto durante un tiempo espec�fico.

methods.name: OcultarTexto
methods.xmlDoc: Oculta el texto ajustando su escala a (0, 0, 0).

types.name: ConfiguracionGlobal
types.xmlDoc: Almacena configuraciones globales para el juego. Este script es est�tico y accesible desde cualquier otro script.

types.name: CoroutineHelper
types.xmlDoc: Singleton helper to run coroutines from non-MonoBehaviour classes or static methods.

types.name: UnitController
types.xmlDoc: Extensión parcial de UnitController con lógica de revivimiento. Este archivo contiene: - Propiedades relacionadas al revivimiento (isDown, revivalTimer, etc.) - Métodos para entrar/salir del estado de caído - Lógica de input del jugador para revivir con barra espaciadora - Detección de aliados cercanos que pueden revivir

methods.name: EnterDamagedState
methods.xmlDoc: El soldado entra en estado caído (HP <= 0)

methods.name: ExitDamagedState
methods.xmlDoc: El soldado sale del estado caído (fue revivido)

methods.name: IsDown
methods.xmlDoc: Verifica si el soldado está caído

methods.name: UpdateLeaderRevivalInput
methods.xmlDoc: Ejecutado desde LiderandoState.Update() El líder intenta revivir a un aliado cercano caído presionando barra espaciadora

methods.name: FindClosestDamagedAlly
methods.xmlDoc: Busca el aliado caído más cercano al rango de revivimiento

methods.name: CanReviveAlly
methods.xmlDoc: Verifica si este aliado puede revivar a un compañero caído. Retorna true si está cerca, no está atacando y el objetivo está caído.

methods.name: StartRevivingAlly
methods.xmlDoc: Inicia el revivimiento de un aliado (para aliados IA, no el líder)

methods.name: ModifyTakeDamageForRevival
methods.xmlDoc: Modificado: Cuando HP llega a 0, entra en estado caído en lugar de morir.

fields.name: _currentHelpPriority
fields.xmlDoc: Prioridad del objetivo actual de ayuda (0 = ninguno, 1 = líder, 2 = aliado)

methods.name: ResetHelpPriority
methods.xmlDoc: Resetea la prioridad de ayuda cuando se vuelve a formación.

methods.name: OnHelpRequested
methods.xmlDoc: Responde a pedidos de ayuda del escuadrón. Prioridad 1 (líder) siempre reemplaza prioridad 2 (aliado). No se auto-ayuda ni ayuda si está liderando.

methods.name: AlertFromExplosion
methods.xmlDoc: Recibe alerta de explosión cercana y va a investigar la zona.

types.name: TickManager
types.xmlDoc: Singleton de ticks temporales. Reparte tres frecuencias de actualización para evitar correr lógica costosa cada frame. Asignarse en OnEnable del GameObject que lo contenga. USO: OnEnable → TickManager.Instance.OnTick_0_5s += MiMetodo; OnDisable → TickManager.Instance.OnTick_0_5s -= MiMetodo;

types.name: RevivingState
types.xmlDoc: ESTADO: REVIVIENDO El aliado está cerca de un compañero caído y puede revivir. Si está cercano, no está atacando/siendo atacado, va directamente a revivir. Una vez que comienza el revivimiento, mantiene el contacto por 3 segundos. Si se aleja o el caído se mueve, el revivimiento se cancela.

types.name: RevivalSystemSetup
types.xmlDoc: Script helper para configurar automáticamente el sistema de revivimiento. Agrega este script a cada soldado en el inspector y presiona el botón de Setup.

methods.name: SetupRevivalSystem
methods.xmlDoc: Método que se ejecuta desde el Inspector (botón)

types.name: RedesSceneCreator
types.xmlDoc: Tools > Redes > 1. Create Scene Builds the full primitive scene STRUCTURE (camera, legacy-Text UI, and all the system GameObjects with their components attached). It only creates the hierarchy + components; wiring the serialized references is done by "3. Link & Assign All".

types.name: RedesProceduralAudio
types.xmlDoc: Creates procedural audio clips for VFX/SFX that don't have external audio files. Called by the Corregir pipeline to ensure all sounds exist.

methods.name: EnsureAudioClips
methods.xmlDoc: Ensures both procedural audio clips exist. Safe to call multiple times.

methods.name: CreateOuchClip
methods.xmlDoc: Creates a short descending-pitch sine wave that mimics a human grunt/ouch. Duration: ~0.25 seconds, starts at 400Hz descends to 200Hz.

methods.name: CreateObstacleHitClip
methods.xmlDoc: Creates a short metallic impact / white noise burst. Duration: ~0.15 seconds.

methods.name: WriteWav
methods.xmlDoc: Writes a mono 16-bit PCM WAV file.

types.name: RedesPrefabCreator
types.xmlDoc: Tools > Redes > 2. Create Prefabs Creates the primitive prefabs (Player capsule + Bullet sphere) with ALL their network components attached. References between components on the SAME prefab are wired here; cross-asset wiring (bullet prefab into the player, etc.) is done by "3. Link & Assign All".

methods.name: GetOrCreateAnimator
methods.xmlDoc: Public entry-point so the test scene builder can reuse this.

methods.name: AssignArrayRefs
methods.xmlDoc: Asigna un array de AudioClips a un campo serializado de tipo AudioClip[].

types.name: RedesBuildAll
types.xmlDoc: Invocado desde línea de comandos por BuildAndTest.bat Hace en un solo proceso de Unity: 1. Crea/regenera la escena RedesGame 2. Crea los prefabs Player y Bullet 3. Enlaza todas las referencias 4. Agrega RedesGame a BuildSettings 5. Compila y construye el juego para Windows x64

methods.name: EnsureRedesSceneIsFirst
methods.xmlDoc: Asegura que RedesGame sea la ÚNICA escena del build dedicado de Redes, cargada en índice 0. No toca el orden del proyecto principal.

types.name: Projectile
types.xmlDoc: Bullet as a Network Object (assignment requirement: Network Object). Spawned by PlayerShooting on the server. Moves forward and, on hit, calls IDamageable.TakeDamage (depends on the abstraction, not on the player - SOLID/DIP). Logic is implemented by another agent.

types.name: ObstacleHitFeedback
types.xmlDoc: Component purely for visual feedback (squash & stretch) when an obstacle is hit by a projectile.

types.name: IDamageable
types.xmlDoc: SOLID (Interface Segregation + Dependency Inversion): Anything that can be hit implements this. Projectile depends on this abstraction, NOT on PlayerHealth directly, so new damageable things (crates, turrets) can be added without touching combat code.

methods.name: TakeDamage
methods.xmlDoc: Apply damage. 'attacker' lets the match system know who scored the hit (needed for the win/lose notification). Logic is implemented by another agent.

types.name: DestructibleBombObstacle
types.xmlDoc: Network-enabled combustible bomb obstacle (3D Cube).

types.name: RedesLoadingScreen
types.xmlDoc: Singleton Screen Manager that provides a full-screen loading overlay to block inputs during network connection, scene restarting, and match ending.

types.name: PlayerController
types.xmlDoc: MVC - CONTROLLER that binds the LOCAL player's networked state to the HUD. Lives in the scene. When the local NetworkPlayer spawns it calls Bind(), then this controller keeps the GameHudView in sync with the PlayerModel. Keeps the View free of any game logic (SRP). Logic is implemented by another agent.

methods.name: Bind
methods.xmlDoc: Called by the local NetworkPlayer when it spawns.

types.name: MatchController
types.xmlDoc: MVC - CONTROLLER for the match outcome (win/lose). It receives the LOCAL result (already resolved per-client by the networked MatchNetworkController RPC) and drives the ResultView. It also relays the ResultView's Action so the GameFlowController can move to Finished. Logic is implemented by another agent.

methods.name: NotifyResult
methods.xmlDoc: Entry point called per-client with this client's outcome. Shows the view (which also fires the Action + the required logs).

types.name: EntityDisplayManager
types.xmlDoc: MVC - CONTROLLER that manages a pool of 20 health sliders on the Canvas, converting the world positions of active entities (players/enemies) to screen space. Scalable and decoupled from concrete player implementations via IEntityDisplayModel.

types.name: RedesLog
types.xmlDoc: Centralized, flagged debug logger for the whole "Redes" project. WHY THIS EXISTS (SOLID - Single Responsibility): All gameplay/network code logs THROUGH this class so the console shows consistent, colored flags. The assignment requires seeing very clear debug logs, so every system uses one of the channels below. The actual log STRINGS required by the assignment live where the event happens (network, lobby, match, player). This class only formats them.

types.name: GameEventBus
types.xmlDoc: GLOBAL EVENT BUS Desacopla la lógica de red de las respuestas visuales/auditivas, permitiendo que cualquier sistema (como la UI, Audio o el GameManager) se entere de eventos clave sin necesidad de referencias cruzadas directas.

types.name: GamePhase
types.xmlDoc: High level phases of the game flow (used by the MVC Model + Controllers). The GameFlowController moves the GameStateModel through these phases.

types.name: MatchResult
types.xmlDoc: Result of the match from the perspective of a single client. Notified to every user (win/lose condition requirement).

types.name: GameConstants
types.xmlDoc: Single source of truth for tuning values shared across systems. Keeping them here (instead of magic numbers) follows SOLID: every system depends on this abstraction, not on scattered literals.

types.name: RagdollController
types.xmlDoc: Procedurally registers bones on Start, adds capsule/sphere colliders and rigidbodies, and provides a simple API to enable/disable Ragdoll physics. Works for both online (NetworkPlayer) and offline (OfflinePlayerTester / DummyEnemy) characters.

methods.name: SetLocalPlayerReady
methods.xmlDoc: Called by MatchController when the local player clicks "Retry". Sends to the server that we are ready.

methods.name: NotifyReturnToLobby
methods.xmlDoc: Called by MatchController when this local player wants to return to the lobby. Broadcasts to the other client so they know they are disconnected/returning to lobby.

types.name: PlayerModel
types.xmlDoc: MVC - MODEL for a single player's gameplay data (health + ammo). Pure data; the networked source of truth lives in the NetworkBehaviours, this is the local view-model the HUD binds to.

types.name: IEntityDisplayModel
types.xmlDoc: Model interface representing an entity whose status/health can be displayed in the UI overlay. Scalable to support other properties in the future (e.g. ammo, name, energy).

types.name: GameStateModel
types.xmlDoc: MVC - MODEL. Pure data + state for the overall game flow. No Unity, no Fusion: just state and change notifications (Observer). Controllers mutate it; Views read it / listen to it.

types.name: RoomSessionHandler
types.xmlDoc: SOLID/SRP: the ONLY thing this class decides is "create a new room" vs. "join an existing one", based on the session list.

types.name: PlayerSpawner
types.xmlDoc: SOLID/SRP: only responsibility = spawn / despawn the player NetworkObject. In a HOST architecture, ONLY the server (host) is allowed to Spawn. Keeps a map of PlayerRef -> spawned NetworkObject so it can despawn on leave. Logic is implemented by another agent.

methods.name: SpawnPlayer
methods.xmlDoc: Spawns one player. Called by HostNetworkService.OnPlayerJoined (server only).

types.name: ISessionListHandler
types.xmlDoc: SOLID (Single Responsibility): isolates the "what do we do with the session list" decision (create a new room vs. join an existing one) away from the runner callbacks plumbing. Logic is implemented by another agent.

methods.name: HandleSessionList
methods.xmlDoc: Called whenever Fusion reports the available sessions. Decides whether to CREATE a room (0 sessions) or JOIN one (X sessions).

fields.name: DelayIfSceneManagerIsBusy
fields.xmlDoc: If enabled, the provider will delay acquiring a prefab instance if the scene manager is busy.

types.name: FusionBootstrapDebugGUI
types.xmlDoc: Companion component for . Automatically added as needed for rendering in-game networking IMGUI.

fields.name: EnableHotkeys
fields.xmlDoc: When enabled, the in-game user interface buttons can be activated with the keys H (Host), S (Server) and C (Client).

fields.name: BaseSkin
fields.xmlDoc: The GUISkin to use as the base for the scalable in-game UI.

types.name: FusionBootstrap
types.xmlDoc: A Fusion prototyping class for starting up basic networking. Add this component to your startup scene, and supply a . Can be set to automatically startup the network, display an in-game menu, or allow simplified start calls like .

fields.name: RunnerPrefab
fields.xmlDoc: Supply a Prefab or a scene object which has the component on it, as well as any runner dependent components which implement , such as or your own custom INetworkInput implementations.

fields.name: StartMode
fields.xmlDoc: Select how network startup will be triggered. Automatically, by in-game menu selection, or exclusively by script.

fields.name: AutoStartAs
fields.xmlDoc: When is set to , this option selects if the will be started as a dedicated server, or as a host (which is a server with a local player).

fields.name: AutoHideGUI
fields.xmlDoc: will not render GUI elements while == .

fields.name: AutoClients
fields.xmlDoc: The number of client instances that will be created if running in Mulit-Peer Mode. When using the Select start mode, this number will be the default value for the additional clients option box.

fields.name: ClientStartDelay
fields.xmlDoc: How long to wait after starting a peer before starting the next one.

fields.name: ServerPort
fields.xmlDoc: The port that server/host will use.

fields.name: DefaultRoomName
fields.xmlDoc: The default room name to use when connecting to photon cloud.

fields.name: InitialScenePath
fields.xmlDoc: The Scene that will be loaded after network shutdown completes (all peers have disconnected). If this field is null or invalid, will be set to the current scene when runs Awake().

fields.name: _currentStage
fields.xmlDoc: Indicates which step of the startup process is currently in.

fields.name: AutoConnectVirtualInstances
fields.xmlDoc: Requires Multiplayer Play Mode (MPPM) to be installed. If enabled, will automatically join the virtual instance.

fields.name: VirtualInstanceConnectDelay
fields.xmlDoc: How much to wait before the main instance lets the virtual instances connect.

properties.name: CurrentStage
properties.xmlDoc: Indicates which step of the startup process is currently in.

properties.name: LastCreatedClientIndex
properties.xmlDoc: The index number used for the last created peer.

properties.name: CurrentServerMode
properties.xmlDoc: The server mode that was used for initial startup. Used to inform UI which client modes should be available.

properties.name: ShouldShowGUI
properties.xmlDoc: Only show the GUI if the StartMode is set to UserInterface and not being run in a Virtual Instance (MPPM).

methods.name: StartSinglePlayer
methods.xmlDoc: Start a single player instance.

methods.name: StartServer
methods.xmlDoc: Start a server instance.

methods.name: StartHost
methods.xmlDoc: Start a host instance. This is a server instance, with a local player.

methods.name: StartClient
methods.xmlDoc: Start a client instance.

methods.name: StartServerPlusClients
methods.xmlDoc: Start a Fusion server instance, and the number of client instances indicated by . InstanceMode must be set to Multi-Peer mode, as this requires multiple instances.

methods.name: StartHostPlusClients
methods.xmlDoc: Start a Fusion host instance, and the number of client instances indicated by . InstanceMode must be set to Multi-Peer mode, as this requires multiple instances.

methods.name: StartServerPlusClients
methods.xmlDoc: Start a Fusion server instance, and the indicated number of client instances. InstanceMode must be set to Multi-Peer mode, as this requires multiple instances.

methods.name: StartHostPlusClients
methods.xmlDoc: Start a Fusion host instance (server with local player), and the indicated number of additional client instances. InstanceMode must be set to Multi-Peer mode, as this requires multiple instances.

methods.name: StartMultipleClients
methods.xmlDoc: Start a Fusion host instance (server with local player), and the indicated number of additional client instances. InstanceMode must be set to Multi-Peer mode, as this requires multiple instances.

methods.name: StartMultipleSharedClients
methods.xmlDoc: Start as Room on the Photon cloud, and connects as one or more clients.

types.name: StartModes
types.xmlDoc: Selection for how will behave at startup.

types.name: Stage
types.xmlDoc: The current stage of connection or shutdown.

types.name: FusionBasicBillboard
types.xmlDoc: Component which automatically faces this GameObject toward the supplied Camera. If Camera == null, will face towards Camera.main.

fields.name: Camera
fields.xmlDoc: Force a particular camera to billboard this object toward. Leave null to use Camera.main.

types.name: NetworkAssetSourceResource
types.xmlDoc: Resources-based implementation of the asset source pattern.

fields.name: ResourcePath
fields.xmlDoc: Resource path. Note that this is a Unity resource path, not a file path.

fields.name: SubObjectName
fields.xmlDoc: Sub-object name. If empty, the main object is loaded.

properties.name: IsCompleted
properties.xmlDoc: Returns if the asset is loaded.

properties.name: Description
properties.xmlDoc: The description of the asset source. Used for debugging.

methods.name: Acquire
methods.xmlDoc: Loads the asset. In synchronous mode, the asset is loaded immediately. In asynchronous mode, the asset is loaded in the background.

methods.name: Release
methods.xmlDoc: Unloads the asset. If the asset is not loaded, an exception is thrown. If the asset is loaded multiple times, it is only unloaded when the last acquire is released.

methods.name: WaitForResult
methods.xmlDoc: Blocks until the asset is loaded. If the asset is not loaded, an exception is thrown.

types.name: NetworkAssetSourceStatic
types.xmlDoc: Hard reference-based implementation of the asset source pattern. This asset source forms a hard reference to the asset and never releases it. This type is meant to be used at runtime. For edit-time, prefer , as it delays actually loading the asset, improving the editor performance.

fields.name: Object
fields.xmlDoc: The asset reference. Can point to an asset or to a runtime-created object.

properties.name: IsCompleted
properties.xmlDoc: Returns .

methods.name: Acquire
methods.xmlDoc: Does nothing, the asset is always loaded.

methods.name: Release
methods.xmlDoc: Does nothing, the asset is always loaded.

methods.name: WaitForResult
methods.xmlDoc: Returns or throws an exception if the reference is missing.

types.name: NetworkAssetSourceStaticLazy
types.xmlDoc: An edit-time optimised version of , taking advantage of Unity's lazy loading of assets. At runtime, this type behaves exactly like , except for the inability to use runtime-created objects.

fields.name: Object
fields.xmlDoc: The asset reference. Can only point to an asset, runtime-created objects will not work.

types.name: FusionGlobalScriptableObjectAddressAttribute
types.xmlDoc: If applied at the assembly level, allows to be loaded with Addressables.

properties.name: Address
properties.xmlDoc: The address to load from.

methods.name: FusionGlobalScriptableObjectAddressAttribute
methods.xmlDoc: The type this attribute will attempt to load. The address to load from.

methods.name: Load
methods.xmlDoc: Loads the asset from the . Uses WaitForCompletion internally, so platforms that do not support it need to preload the address prior to loading.

types.name: FusionGlobalScriptableObjectResourceAttribute
types.xmlDoc: If applied at the assembly level, allows to be loaded with Resources. There is a default registration for this attribute, which attempts to load the asset from Resources using path from .

properties.name: ResourcePath
properties.xmlDoc: Path in Resources.

properties.name: InstantiateIfLoadedInEditor
properties.xmlDoc: If loaded in the editor, should the result be instantiated instead of returning the asset itself? The default is .

methods.name: FusionGlobalScriptableObjectResourceAttribute
methods.xmlDoc: The type this attribute will attempt to load. Resources path or /empty if path from is to be used.

methods.name: Load
methods.xmlDoc: Loads the asset from Resources synchronously.

types.name: FusionUnityLogger
types.xmlDoc: Fusion logger implementation for Unity.

fields.name: LogActiveRunnerTick
fields.xmlDoc: Is true, the active runner's tick will be logged.

types.name: JsonUtilityExtensions
types.xmlDoc: Extends capabilities of by adding type annotations to the serialized JSON, Unity object reference handling and integer enquotement.

methods.name: EnquoteIntegers
methods.xmlDoc: Enquotes integers in the JSON string that are at least long. This is useful for parsers that interpret large integers as floating point numbers.

methods.name: ToJsonWithTypeAnnotation
methods.xmlDoc: Converts the object to JSON with type annotations.

methods.name: ToJsonWithTypeAnnotation
methods.xmlDoc: Converts the object/IList to JSON with type annotations.

methods.name: FromJsonWithTypeAnnotation
methods.xmlDoc: Converts JSON with type annotation to an instance of . If the JSON contains type annotations, they need to match the expected result type. If there are no type annotations, use to return the expected type.

methods.name: FromJsonWithTypeAnnotation
methods.xmlDoc: Converts JSON with type annotation. If there are no type annotations, use to return the expected type.

types.name: FusionLogInitializer
types.xmlDoc: Initializes the logging system for Fusion. Use to completely override the log level and trace channels or to provide a custom logger. Use to override default Unity logger settings.

methods.name: Initialize
methods.xmlDoc: Initializes the logging system for Fusion. This method is called automatically when the assembly is loaded.

types.name: FusionMppmStatus
types.xmlDoc: The current status of MPPM. If the package is not enabled, this will always be .

types.name: FusionMppm
types.xmlDoc: Support for Multiplayer Play Mode (MPPM). It uses named pipes to communicate between the main Unity instance and virtual instances.

fields.name: Status
fields.xmlDoc: The current status of MPPM.

fields.name: MainEditor
fields.xmlDoc: If is , this static field can be used to send commands.

methods.name: Send
methods.xmlDoc: Sends a command to all virtual instances. Use as: FusionMppm.MainEditor?.Send

methods.name: Broadcast
methods.xmlDoc: Broadcasts a command to all virtual instances.

types.name: FusionMppmCommand
types.xmlDoc: The base class for all Fusion MPPM commands.

properties.name: NeedsAck
properties.xmlDoc: Does the main instance need to wait for an ack?

properties.name: PersistentKey
properties.xmlDoc: If the command is persistent (i.e. needs to be executed on each domain reload), this key is used to store it.

methods.name: Execute
methods.xmlDoc: Execute the command on a virtual instance.

types.name: FusionUnityExtensions
types.xmlDoc: Provides backwards compatibility for Unity API.

methods.name: PostprocessBehaviour
methods.xmlDoc: Postprocesses the behaviour. Returns true if the object was marked dirty.

types.name: FusionScalableIMGUI
types.xmlDoc: In-Game IMGUI style used for the interface.

methods.name: GetScaledSkin
methods.xmlDoc: Get the custom scalable skin, already resized to the current screen. Provides the height, width, padding and margin used.

methods.name: ScaleGuiSkinToScreenHeight
methods.xmlDoc: Modifies a skin to make it scale with screen height.

methods.name: GetComponents
methods.xmlDoc: Finds all components of type in the scene.

methods.name: GetComponents
methods.xmlDoc: Finds all components of type in the scene.

methods.name: GetComponents
methods.xmlDoc: Finds all components of type in the scene.

methods.name: FindComponent
methods.xmlDoc: Finds the first instance of type in the scene. Returns null if no instance found.

fields.name: RecognizedBehaviourNames
fields.xmlDoc: Types that fusion.runtime isn't aware of, which need to be found using names instead.

fields.name: CommonObjectLookup
fields.xmlDoc: Dictionary lookup for manually added visibility nodes (which indicates only one instance should be visible at a time), which returns a list of nodes for a given LocalIdentifierInFile.

methods.name: AddVisibilityNodes
methods.xmlDoc: Find all component types that contribute to a scene rendering, and associate them with a component, and add them to the runner's list of visibility nodes.

methods.name: RefreshRunnerVisibility
methods.xmlDoc: Reapplies a runner's IsVisibile setting to all of its registered visibility nodes.

types.name: ShotImpactBus
types.xmlDoc: Bus de eventos estático para impactos de disparos en el mundo. Cualquier proyectil (Proyectil.cs, Bala.cs) llama Trigger() al impactar. Enemigos y aliados se suscriben para reaccionar a ruidos de disparo cercanos. Flujo: Proyectil impacta → ShotImpactBus.Trigger(pos, owner) EnemyController.OnShotNearby() filtra por distancia + LOS y reacciona AllyResponseSystem.OnShotNearby() filtra por distancia + LOS y reacciona

types.name: NetworkPlayer
types.xmlDoc: Root NetworkBehaviour for a player (the prefab's "brain"/facade). SOLID/SRP: it does NOT implement movement/shooting/health itself; it just holds references to the sibling systems and exposes the local-player hook. Each concrete system (movement, shooting, health, ammo, animation) is its own component so they can change independently. This is a Network Object (the prefab carries a NetworkObject component). Logic is implemented by another agent.

types.name: AmmoSystem
types.xmlDoc: EXTRA MECHANIC (distinct from Movement / Shoot / Jump): AMMO + RELOAD. Magazine is limited; firing consumes ammo; when empty the player must reload (takes RELOAD_TIME). Ammo + reload state are [Networked] so they stay in sync across host and clients (no desfase). Logic is implemented by another agent.

methods.name: TryConsume
methods.xmlDoc: Consume one round. Returns false if empty.

methods.name: StartReload
methods.xmlDoc: Begin reloading.

types.name: TestSceneManager
types.xmlDoc: Manages the test scene HUD: kill counter, debug panel, controls legend.

types.name: PlayerInputMode
types.xmlDoc: Offline player controller for the test scene. Directly drives movement, shooting and animates the player WITHOUT Fusion networking — purely for local input/animation/VFX testing. Handles death with ragdoll activation via EventBus.

types.name: OfflineBullet
types.xmlDoc: Component added at runtime in the offline test scene to animate the bullet prefab's movement and handle offline trigger hits on DummyEnemy.

types.name: DummyEnemy
types.xmlDoc: Offline dummy enemy for the test scene. Takes damage, shows health, dies with Ragdoll, and respawns. Completely independent of Fusion networking.

types.name: CameraFollow
types.xmlDoc: Simple script to make the camera follow a target horizontally without copying its rotation (crucial for top-down mouse look).

types.name: ChangeDllManager
types.xmlDoc: Provides methods to toggle between different DLL modes for the Fusion framework.

methods.name: ChangeDllModeToSharedDebug
methods.xmlDoc: Changes the DLL mode to Debug.

methods.name: ChangeDllModeToSharedRelease
methods.xmlDoc: Changes the DLL mode to Release.

methods.name: ChangeDllMode
methods.xmlDoc: Changes the DLL mode based on the specified build type and build mode.

methods.name: GetValueFromFieldName
parameters: BindingFlags bindings = BindingFlags.Instance | BindingFlags.Static | BindingFlags.Public | BindingFlags.NonPublic

methods.name: EnsureComponentExists
methods.xmlDoc: Ensure GameObject has component T. Will create as needed and return the found/created component.

methods.name: CreatePrimitive
methods.xmlDoc: Create a scene object with all of the supplied arguments and parameters applied.

types.name: INetworkAssetSourceFactory
types.xmlDoc: A factory that creates asset source instances for a given asset.

properties.name: Order
properties.xmlDoc: The order in which this factory is executed. The lower the number, the earlier it is executed.

types.name: NetworkAssetSourceFactoryContext
types.xmlDoc: A context object that is passed to instances to create an asset source instance.

fields.name: InstanceID
fields.xmlDoc: Asset instance ID.

fields.name: AssetGuid
fields.xmlDoc: Asset Unity GUID;

fields.name: AssetName
fields.xmlDoc: Asset name;

fields.name: IsMainAsset
fields.xmlDoc: Is this the main asset.

properties.name: AssetPath
properties.xmlDoc: Asset Unity path.

properties.name: Object
properties.xmlDoc: The object pointed to be

methods.name: NetworkAssetSourceFactoryContext
methods.xmlDoc: Create a new instance of .

methods.name: NetworkAssetSourceFactoryContext
methods.xmlDoc: Create a new instance of .

methods.name: NetworkAssetSourceFactoryContext
methods.xmlDoc: Create a new instance of .

types.name: NetworkAssetSourceFactoryResource
types.xmlDoc: A implementation that creates instances for assets in the Resources folder.

types.name: FusionCustomDependency
types.xmlDoc: A wrapper around Unity's custom dependencies. Allows refresh to be deferred (if circumstances permit) and works around issues with custom dependencies in MPPM.

fields.xmlDoc: Global force immediate switch. Set to true to force all the refreshes to be synchronous.

methods.name: FusionCustomDependency

methods.name: Refresh
methods.xmlDoc: Refreshes the dependency. Under normal circumstances, this will enqueue the operation until the next . The hash will be calculated immediately if any of these is true: - - - Note that if returns true, the immediate refresh will result with an error.

methods.name: GetGlobalAssetPath
methods.xmlDoc: Locates the asset that is going to be used as a global asset for the given type, that is an asset marked with the label. If there are multiple such assets, exception is thrown. If there are no such assets, empty string is returned.

methods.name: TryGetGlobalAssetPath
methods.xmlDoc: A wrapper around that returns a value indicating if it was able to find the asset.

methods.name: TryImportGlobal

methods.name: GetMemberIncludingBaseTypes
methods.xmlDoc: Returns the first found member of the given name. Includes private members.

methods.name: GetFieldIncludingBaseTypes
methods.xmlDoc: Normal reflection GetField() won't find private fields in parents (only will find protected). So this recurses the hard to find privates. This is needed since Unity serialization does find inherited privates.

methods.name: DrawNetworkObjectCheck

methods.name: GetHashesAndNames

methods.name: GetWordCount

types.name: NetworkProjectConfigUtilities
types.xmlDoc: Editor utilities for creating and managing the singleton.

types.name: PlayerController
types.xmlDoc: Controlador principal del jugador (MVC - Controller). Coordina la entrada física (Input), la actualización del modelo (CharacterModel) y la vista (CharacterView). Preparado para integración futura con Photon mediante la bandera 'hasInputAuthority'.

methods.name: ActivarTodosLosComponentes
methods.xmlDoc: Activa todos los componentes de un GameObject.

types.name: Enemigo
types.xmlDoc: Gestiona el comportamiento del enemigo. El enemigo dispara, recibe da�o y tiene una barra de vida. Desaparece al recibir un disparo.

methods.name: Disparar
methods.xmlDoc: Gestiona el disparo del enemigo.

methods.name: RecibirDanio
methods.xmlDoc: Aplica da�o al enemigo.

methods.name: Morir
methods.xmlDoc: Destruye al enemigo cuando su vida llega a cero.

types.name: RenderSimStats
types.xmlDoc: List of all simulation stats able to render on a graph.

types.name: CanvasAnchor
types.xmlDoc: The side to attach the statistics panel anchor.

properties.name: IsPanelActive
properties.xmlDoc: Gets a value indicating whether the statistics panel is active.

methods.name: SetStatsCustomConfig
methods.xmlDoc: Sets the custom configuration for Fusion Statistics.

methods.name: SetCanvasAnchor
methods.xmlDoc: Sets the anchor position of the Fusion Statistics canvas.

methods.name: OnEditorChange
methods.xmlDoc: Called from a custom editor script. Will update any editor information into the fusion statistics.

methods.name: SetupStatisticsPanel
methods.xmlDoc: Sets up the statistics panel for Fusion statistic tracking.

methods.name: SetWorldAnchor
methods.xmlDoc: Sets the world anchor for Fusion Statistics. Set null to return to screen space overlay.

methods.name: DestroyStatisticsPanel
methods.xmlDoc: Destroys the statistics panel.

properties.name: NetworkObjectID
properties.xmlDoc: Gets the unique identifier of the network object.

types.name: RunnerVisibilityLinksRoot
types.xmlDoc: Flag component which indicates a NetworkObject has already been factored into a Runner's VisibilityNode list.

types.name: IRunnerVisibilityRecognizedType
types.xmlDoc: Flags a MonoBehaviour class as a RunnerVisibilityControl recognized type. Will be included in runner visibility handling, and will be found by component finds.

types.name: RunnerVisibilityLink
types.xmlDoc: Identifies visible/audible components (such as renderers, canvases, lights) that should be enabled/disabled by runner visibility handling. Automatically added to scene objects and spawned objects during play if running in . Additionally this component can be added manually at development time to identify specific Behaviours or Renderers you would like to restrict to one enabled copy at a time.

fields.name: PreferredRunner
fields.xmlDoc: If more than one runner instance is visible, this indicates which peer's clone of this entity should be visible.

fields.name: Component
fields.xmlDoc: The associated component with this node. This Behaviour or Renderer will be enabled/disabled when its NetworkRunner.IsVisible value is changed.

fields.name: Guid
fields.xmlDoc: Guid is used for common objects (user flagged components that should only run in one instance), to identify matching clones.

properties.name: DefaultState
properties.xmlDoc: Set to false to indicate that this object should remain disabled even when is set to true.

methods.name: SetEnabled
methods.xmlDoc: Sets the visibility state of this node.

types.name: PreferredRunners
types.xmlDoc: The peer runner that will be used if more than one runner is visible, and this node was manually added by developer (indicating only one instance should be visible at a time).

types.name: RunnerEnableVisibility
types.xmlDoc: When running in Multi-Peer mode, this component automatically will register the associated with , and will automatically attach loaded scene objects and spawned objects with the peers visibility handling.

types.name: RunnerAOIGizmos
types.xmlDoc: Add this Component to the NetworkRunner Prefab or GameObject. If Interest Management is enabled in NetworkProjectConfig ReplicationFeatures, gizmos will be shown that indicate active Area Of Interest cells. These gizmos are currently NOT applicable to Shared Mode and will only render for the Server/Host peer.

types.name: EnableOnSingleRunner
types.xmlDoc: Automatically adds a for each indicated component. These indicated components will be limited to no more than one enabled instance when running in Multi-Peer mode.

fields.name: PreferredRunner
fields.xmlDoc: If more than one runner instance is visible, this indicates which peer's clone of this entity should be visible.

fields.name: Components
fields.xmlDoc: Collection of components that will be marked for Multi-Peer mode as objects that should only have one enabled instance.

fields.name: _guid
fields.xmlDoc: Prefix for the GUIDs of components which are added at runtime.

methods.name: AddNodes
methods.xmlDoc: At runtime startup, this adds a for each component reference to this GameObject.

methods.name: FindRecognizedTypes
methods.xmlDoc: Finds visual/audio components on this GameObject, and adds them to the Components collection.

methods.name: FindNestedRecognizedTypes
methods.xmlDoc: Finds visual/audio nested components on this GameObject and its children, and adds them to the Components collection.

methods.name: RequestPath
methods.xmlDoc: [MODIFICADO] Solicita un camino, probando múltiples nodos para encontrar el de menor COSTO total. Ahora incluye un 'stopOffset' opcional para detenerse antes del destino.

methods.name: GetOffsetTarget
methods.xmlDoc: [NUEVO] Calcula una posición 'offset' unidades *antes* de 'to', viniendo desde 'from'.

methods.name: FindKClosestVisibleNodes
methods.xmlDoc: [NUEVO] Encuentra los K nodos más cercanos a una posición que tienen línea de visión directa. (Esta función no cambia)

methods.name: IsAvoidable
methods.xmlDoc: Comprueba si el objeto tiene una escala cercana a 0.7 (en cualquier eje)

methods.name: CalculateAvoidancePoint
methods.xmlDoc: Calcula un punto lateral para esquivar el objeto

methods.name: DrawDebugCircle
methods.xmlDoc: Dibuja un círculo de debug en el mundo

types.name: LobbyView
types.xmlDoc: MVC - VIEW para el lobby. Muestra dos botones explícitos: "Crear Sala" y "Unirse a Sala". El GameFlowController decide cuál acción ejecutar.

methods.name: ShowButtons
methods.xmlDoc: Muestra solo los botones de selección (estado inicial).

methods.name: HideButtons
methods.xmlDoc: Oculta botones mientras se conecta / espera.

types.name: GameHudView
types.xmlDoc: MVC - VIEW for the in-match HUD (local player health + ammo + teleport cooldown). ONLY draws UI. Binds to PlayerModel via PlayerController. Uses legacy UnityEngine.UI.Text.

methods.name: ShowTeleportCooldown
methods.xmlDoc: Actualiza el radial de cooldown del teleport. progress = 0 → recargando, 1 → listo.

methods.name: ShowCrouch
methods.xmlDoc: Muestra/oculta indicador visual de crouch.

types.name: EntityDisplayView
types.xmlDoc: MVC - VIEW representing a single UI overlay element (health bar slider). Scalable to display additional information in the future (e.g. name, ammunition).

methods.name: SelectShootClip
methods.xmlDoc: Selecciona el clip de disparo según el PlayerId del jugador (1-indexado).

methods.name: SelectRandomOuchClip
methods.xmlDoc: Selecciona un clip de dolor/impacto de forma aleatoria.

types.name: PlayerAmmoView
types.xmlDoc: UI View for ammunition. Listens to PlayerEventBus for ammo state and animates when shooting/reloading/empty. Completely follows the MVC pattern.

types.name: CustomCursorView
types.xmlDoc: Custom Cursor View following MVC/MVP pattern. Handles custom cursor graphics, scaling/rebound animations on shoot, target/hit color changes, radial loading indicator on reload, and ensures the custom cursor is only active when the mouse is within the game window/screen.

types.name: GEN_Inputs
types.xmlDoc: Administrador central de todas las entradas físicas (teclado y mouse) del juego. Mantiene propiedades públicas legibles por otros sistemas y ofrece eventos con opción de depuración en consola.

types.name: EnemyView
types.xmlDoc: Componente de Vista del enemigo. Se encarga de dibujar la GUI de vida y efectos visuales de daño.

types.name: EnemyModel
types.xmlDoc: Modelo de datos que almacena las propiedades y estadísticas de salud/combate del enemigo. Preparado para el futuro para multijugador en red (Photon) con propiedades de sincronización local/remota.

methods.name: Revivir
methods.xmlDoc: Revive al enemigo restaurando su vida y limpiando el estado de muerte.

types.name: DamagedState
types.xmlDoc: ESTADO: CAIDO (Dañado - Down) El soldado está caído y no puede moverse, disparar ni realizar acciones. Solo puede ser revivido por un aliado cercano (el líder) manteniando barra espaciadora por 3 segundos. Mientras está caído es invisible para los detectores enemigos.

types.name: DamagedStateHandler
types.xmlDoc: Manejador auxiliar para las visualizaciones y lógica del estado caído. Se agrega como componente dinámicamente cuando el soldado entra en DamagedState.

methods.name: TickLento
methods.xmlDoc: L�gica de decisi�n de IA (Se ejecuta 10 veces por segundo, no 60-120)

methods.name: OnRevivalComplete
methods.xmlDoc: Llamado cuando el revivimiento se completa — dispara animación de exhalación.

properties.name: IsDown
properties.xmlDoc: True cuando la salud llego a 0. El soldado esta caido pero puede ser revivido.

properties.name: IsDead
properties.xmlDoc: Alias de IsDown para compatibilidad con codigo existente.

methods.name: ReviveHealth
methods.xmlDoc: Restaura HP al porcentaje de revive configurado.

methods.name: Idx
methods.xmlDoc: Índice del arma acotado al rango válido de cada array para evitar IndexOutOfRange.

types.name: InformacionPersonaje
types.xmlDoc: Gestiona la información del personaje, como salud, munición y recargas. Debe estar asociado al objeto principal del jugador.

types.name: IDaniable
types.xmlDoc: Contrato para cualquier objeto capaz de recibir daño del sistema de proyectiles (balas, cohetes, torretas, etc.). Vive en el namespace GLOBAL a propósito: los proyectiles y sensores la resuelven vía GetComponent<IDaniable>() y varios controladores la implementan como global::IDaniable (Destruible, SoldierController, EnemyController).

methods.name: RecibirDano
methods.xmlDoc: Aplica daño al objeto.

methods.name: ActivarBoostVelocidadTemporal
methods.xmlDoc: Activa un boost de velocidad temporal (por ejemplo, desde un pickup) por una duración en segundos.

types.name: PersecucionEnemigo
types.xmlDoc: Gestiona la persecuci�n del enemigo hacia el jugador. El enemigo se mover� hacia el jugador y se detendr� cuando est� a una distancia espec�fica.

methods.name: PerseguirJugador
methods.xmlDoc: Persigue al jugador y se detiene cuando est� lo suficientemente cerca.

methods.name: MirarJugador
methods.xmlDoc: Hace que el enemigo mire al jugador.

methods.name: UpdateAmmoUI
methods.xmlDoc: Actualiza directamente el texto UI de munición con el formato indicado.

types.name: WeaponController
types.xmlDoc: Controlador principal de armas (MVC - Controller). Coordina la entrada de disparo/recarga, la selección en el WeaponModel y la respuesta visual en WeaponView. Reemplaza completamente a CambioDeArma. Preparado para integración futura con Photon mediante la bandera 'hasInputAuthority'.

types.name: VibracionCamara
types.xmlDoc: Gestiona la vibraci�n de la c�mara cuando el jugador recibe un impacto. Debe estar asociado a la c�mara principal.

methods.name: IniciarVibracion
methods.xmlDoc: Inicia la vibraci�n de la c�mara.

types.name: SistemaPuntaje
types.xmlDoc: Gestiona el puntaje del jugador y verifica si ha ganado. Debe estar asociado a un objeto que persista durante toda la escena.

types.name: INetworkService
types.xmlDoc: Abstracción del servicio de red para desacoplar a los controladores de la librería subyacente (Fusion 2).

types.name: HostNetworkService
types.xmlDoc: ARQUITECTURA DE DOS RUNNERS (robusta). PROBLEMA QUE RESUELVE: En Fusion 2 un NetworkRunner que hizo Shutdown queda MUERTO y no se puede reutilizar. El log mostraba que el runner se apagaba solo tras conectarse al lobby (DisconnectByClientLogic), y cualquier StartGame posterior tiraba excepcion vacia -> ni Host ni Unirse funcionaban. SOLUCION: - _lobbyRunner: runner DEDICADO solo a escuchar la lista de salas. Si se cae mientras seguimos en el menu, se RECREA automaticamente. - _gameRunner: runner FRESCO creado en el momento de Crear/Unirse. Nunca se reutiliza uno muerto. CALLSTACK USER 1 (HOST): Awake → StartLobbyWatch() → CreateRunner("LobbyRunner") → JoinSessionLobby OnSessionListUpdated(lobbyRunner) → 0 salas → OnRoomAvailabilityChanged(false) [click CREAR SALA] StartAsHost() → ShutdownLobbyRunner() (intencional) → CreateRunner("GameRunner") → StartGame(Host) → OK → OnHostStarted → WaitingForPlayers OnPlayerJoined(gameRunner, p1=host) → SpawnPlayer → count=1 OnPlayerJoined(gameRunner, p2) → SpawnPlayer → count=2 → OnEnoughPlayersToStart CALLSTACK USER 2 (CLIENT): Awake → StartLobbyWatch() → JoinSessionLobby OnSessionListUpdated → 0 salas → Join DESHABILITADO ...User1 crea sala... OnSessionListUpdated → "RedesRoom" open → OnRoomAvailabilityChanged(true) → Join HABILITADO [click UNIRSE] StartAsClient() → ShutdownLobbyRunner() → CreateRunner("GameRunner") → StartGame(Client) → OK → OnHostStarted → OnConnectedToServer → ambos players spawneados por el host

methods.name: FailAndReturnToLobby
methods.xmlDoc: Limpia el runner de juego fallido y vuelve a mirar el lobby.

types.name: PlayerTeleport
types.xmlDoc: MECÁNICA EXTRA: Teletransporte (Teleport). Al presionar SPACE (y si el cooldown expiró), el jugador se teletransporta a una posición aleatoria dentro de la arena (radio TELEPORT_RANGE). - [Networked] TickTimer TeleportCooldown → cooldown sincronizado en red. - [Networked] Vector3 LastTeleportOrigin / Destination → para VFX en clientes. - OnChangedRender notifica a la vista para mostrar el efecto visual. - Logs con PlayerRef para identificar al actor en todos los clientes.

properties.name: CooldownProgress
properties.xmlDoc: Progreso del cooldown entre 0 (recargando) y 1 (listo).

types.name: PlayerShooting
types.xmlDoc: Shooting (one of the "seen in class" mechanics). Spawns Projectile NetworkObjects on the server (Host architecture). Cooperates with AmmoSystem (extra mechanic): cannot fire with 0 ammo. Logic is implemented by another agent.

methods.name: Fire
methods.xmlDoc: Called from the player's input tick when the fire button is pressed.

types.name: PlayerMovement
types.xmlDoc: Top-down movement (one of the "seen in class" mechanics). NetworkBehaviour so movement is simulated identically on host + clients (no desfase). Logic is implemented by another agent.

types.name: PlayerHealth
types.xmlDoc: Player health. Implements IDamageable (SOLID/DIP) so projectiles damage it without knowing its concrete type. Health is [Networked] => the host is the source of truth, replicated to all clients (no desfase). When health hits 0 it asks the MatchNetworkController to announce the result to EVERYONE. Logic is implemented by another agent.

types.name: PlayerEventBus
types.xmlDoc: Mini Event Bus for a single player instance to decouple player logic from views. Each player prefab has this script, which acts as a dispatcher for player-specific actions.

types.name: PlayerCrouch
types.xmlDoc: MECÁNICA EXTRA: Agacharse (Crouch). El jugador mantiene presionado CTRL para agacharse: - Su escala Y se reduce al 50 % → hitbox más pequeño. - Puede moverse mientras está agachado. - El estado IsCrouching está [Networked] → todos los clientes ven la animación. El input se lee en FixedUpdateNetwork (server-authoritative) para evitar cualquier desfase entre host y cliente.

types.name: Municion
types.xmlDoc: Componente legacy para compatibilidad de munición en prefabs anteriores.

types.name: Manager_VFX
types.xmlDoc: Administrador y Pool de efectos visuales (VFX). Carga automáticamente todos los prefabs de las subcarpetas de VFXPACK y permite instanciarlos mediante nombre.

methods.name: SpawnVFX
methods.xmlDoc: Obtiene un efecto del pool, le cambia la posición e inicia su reproducción. Si el efecto solicitado coincide por nombre, se adapta su prefab temporalmente.

methods.name: LerpCameraSize
methods.xmlDoc: Suaviza el cambio de orthographicSize de la cámara principal.

types.name: RedesTestSceneBuilder
types.xmlDoc: Builds the offline "RedesTest" scene for testing animations, input, VFX, sounds offline. Invoked by Tools > Redes > 6. Crear Escena de Test and also called by Corregir (step 4).

methods.name: BuildUI
methods.returnType: (Text debug, Text kills, Text legend, Text log, Text ammo)

types.name: GameFlowController
types.xmlDoc: CALLSTACK COMPLETO: ── INICIO (ambos jugadores) ────────────────────────────────────────── Awake() → crea GameStateModel, subscribe OnPhaseChanged OnEnable()→ subscribe eventos de red + listeners de botones Start() → HandlePhaseChanged(Booting) → LobbyView visible, botones visibles ── USER 1: CREAR SALA ─────────────────────────────────────────────── CreateRoom() → LobbyView.HideButtons(), ShowStatus("Creando sala...") → NetworkService.StartAsHost() [async, espera Photon] → _model.SetPhase(SearchingSession) ¿StartGame OK? SÍ → OnHostStarted → HandleHostStarted → WaitingForPlayers → "Esperando..." NO → OnConnectionFailed → HandleConnectionFailed → Booting → ShowError OnPlayerCountChanged(1) → LobbyView.ShowPlayerCount(1) OnEnoughPlayersToStart → HandleEnoughPlayers → Playing → HUD visible ── USER 2: UNIRSE ─────────────────────────────────────────────────── JoinRoom() → LobbyView.HideButtons(), ShowStatus("Uniéndose...") → NetworkService.StartAsClient() [async, busca sesion "RedesRoom"] → _model.SetPhase(SearchingSession) ¿StartGame OK? SÍ → OnHostStarted → HandleHostStarted → WaitingForPlayers NO → OnConnectionFailed → vuelve a Booting + error OnEnoughPlayersToStart → Playing

types.name: TeleportCooldownView
types.xmlDoc: MVC - VIEW del cooldown radial de teleporte. Principio de responsabilidad única (SRP): - Lee CooldownProgress del modelo (PlayerTeleport) en Update. - Escucha OnTeleport del PlayerEventBus para disparar la animación "uso". - NUNCA llama a lógica de negocio; solo dibuja la UI y escala el jugador. Colores: - Amarillo → listo (fillAmount == 1) - Azul → cargando (fillAmount < 1) - Verde → animación de uso (expand → shrink en < 0.5s) Escala del jugador: - Al teleportarse: shrink (0.3s) → grow (0.3s) en la transform del jugador.

methods.name: Bind
methods.xmlDoc: Llamado por PlayerController cuando vincula este View al jugador local.

methods.name: UseAnimCoroutine
methods.xmlDoc: Animación del radial: verde, expande de 1→1.3, luego vuelve a 1, en USE_ANIM_DURATION segundos.

methods.name: PlayerScaleCoroutine
methods.xmlDoc: Animación de escala del jugador: se achica (~0.3x) al inicio y vuelve al 100% al llegar. Total: SCALE_SHRINK_TIME + SCALE_GROW_TIME ≈ 0.45s.

types.name: ResultView
types.xmlDoc: MVC - VIEW for the end-of-match notification (WIN / LOSE). Every user is notified of the result (assignment requirement) and the notification is delivered "con action" (C# Action) so other systems can react too. Uses legacy UnityEngine.UI.Text.

methods.name: ShowResult
methods.xmlDoc: Called by MatchController when the match ends. Shows text AND fires the Action.

fields.name: IsSceneTakeOverEnabled
fields.xmlDoc: If enabled and there is an already loaded scene that matches what the scene manager has intended to load, that scene will be used instead and load will be avoided.

fields.name: LogSceneLoadErrors
fields.xmlDoc: Should all scene load errors be logged into the console? If disabled, errors can still be retrieved via the or .

fields.name: DestroySpawnedPrefabsOnSceneUnload
fields.xmlDoc: If enabled the scenemanager despawns all runtime spawned prefab instances (not scene objects) before unloading a scene. If the peer does not have StateAuthority over the object it is destroyed instead of despawned. If disabled the destroy will be indirectly done via the scene unload from Unity however it will be async and might be delayed, this can lead to the scene change being synchronized in an earlier tick than the destroys.

fields.name: _allOwnedScenes
fields.xmlDoc: All the scenes loaded by all the managers. Used when is enabled.

fields.name: _multiPeerSceneRoots
fields.xmlDoc: In multiple peer mode, each runner maintains its own scene where all the newly loaded scenes are moved to. This is to make sure physics are properly sandboxed.

fields.name: _runningCoroutines
fields.xmlDoc: List of running coroutines. Only one is actually executed at a time.

fields.name: _tempUnloadScene
fields.xmlDoc: For remote clients, this manager first unloads old scenes then loads the new ones. It might happen that all the current scenes need to be unloaded and in such case a temp scene needs to be created to ensure at least one scene loaded at all times.

properties.name: MultiPeerScene
properties.xmlDoc: Scene used when Multiple Peer mode is used. Each loaded scene is merged into this one, allowing for multiple runners to have separate cross-scene physics.

properties.name: MultiPeerDontDestroyOnLoadRoot
properties.xmlDoc: Root for DontDestroyOnLoad objects. Instantiated on .

types.name: RedesSceneLinker
types.xmlDoc: Tools > Redes > 3. Link & Assign All Opens the generated scene and wires EVERY serialized reference between the systems (network controllers views) and drops the prefabs into the fields that need them. Run this AFTER "1. Create Scene" and "2. Create Prefabs". It finds objects by component type / child name, so it is safe to re-run.

methods.name: AssignArrayRefs
methods.xmlDoc: Asigna un array de AudioClips a un campo serializado de tipo AudioClip[].
